title: The Path Matrix Method: A General Closure–Feedback Protocol for the Generative Replica Model
author: Chen, Shu Jian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This work presents the Path Matrix Method, a general protocol for computing closure and feedback within the Generative Replica (GR) model and Inffer Map system. Building on the understanding of Triogenesis, we formalize a path-based representation in which straintity and pure length define invariant structural relationships, and pairwise contrasts are expressed through path matrices. 
 The method decomposes the system into recurrence structures, which are grade-invariant, and hyperstraint thread configurations, which generate order-dependent inputs. Observable closure is defined as a divergence--convergence mapping of these inputs through replica-tier resonance, expressed as sequential path matrix operations. Training is formulated as a structured feedback process that adjusts chaotic modes, constraint compliance, and structural definitions to align generated outputs with reference patterns. 
 From a computational perspective, resonance-governed path matrices exhibit functional equivalence to the trainable matrices of artificial neural networks (ANNs), explaining their effectiveness in pattern representation. However, the GR framework extends beyond conventional ANNs by providing a generative, structurally constrained mechanism in which inputs, mappings, and outputs are unified within a single system. This enables not only pattern fitting, but also reconstruction and exploration of the underlying generative processes. 
 The proposed protocol supports both direct training and integration of existing ANN models, allowing learned matrices to be interpreted, refined, and resolved into structure-governed forms. As such, the GR--Inffer Map system offers a unified and extensible approach to modeling physical constants, mathematical structures, and observable patterns within a single generative framework.
